system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   0.001;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet section (straight duct along x-axis)
    ( -50  25  25 )   // 0
    ( -50  25 -25 )   // 1
    ( -50 -25 -25 )   // 2
    ( -50 -25  25 )   // 3

    (   0  25  25 )   // 4
    (   0  25 -25 )   // 5
    (   0 -25 -25 )   // 6
    (   0 -25  25 )   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend section
    (  25   0  25 )   // 8
    (  25   0 -25 )   // 9
    (  25 -50 -25 )   // 10
    (  25 -50  25 )   // 11

    // Block 2 - outlet straight duct
    (  25  -75  25 )   // 12
    (  25  -75 -25 )   // 13
    (  25 -125 -25 )   // 14
    (  25 -125  25 )   // 15

    ( -500  -75  25 )   // 16
    ( -500  -75 -25 )   // 17
    ( -500 -125 -25 )   // 18
    ( -500 -125  25 )   // 19
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section
    hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend section
    hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2)
    (10 10 10)
    simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Outlet straight section
    hex (12 16 17 13 15 19 18 14) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 19 18 17)
        );
    }

    walls
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 walls
            (0 4 5 1)
            (3 7 6 2)
            (0 3 7 4)
            (1 5 6 2)

            // Block 1 walls
            (7 8 9 6)
            (3 11 10 2)
            (8 11 10 9)
            (7 3 2 6)

            // Block 2 walls
            (12 16 17 13)
            (15 19 18 14)
            (12 15 19 16)
            (13 14 18 17)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) is inside-out

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run2/runs/squareBend/1/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 46.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 100.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block 0 is "inside-out"

**Error Message:**
```
Block hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) ... is inside-out
```

**Diagnosis:**

The first hex block definition uses vertex ordering `(0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2)` which produces a negative volume (the face normals point inward instead of outward). This means the block is inside-out and cannot be meshed.

Looking at the vertex coordinates:
- Vertex 0: (-50, 25, 25)
- Vertex 4: (0, 25, 25)
- Vertex 5: (0, 25, -25)
- Vertex 1: (-50, 25, -25)

This face `(0 4 5 1)` is the bottom face. For a hex block, the right-hand rule applies: when looking at a face from outside the block, vertices should go counterclockwise. The issue is that the top/bottom face winding needs to be corrected so that the normal points **into** the block (not out).

**Fix:** Swap the winding order of each hex block's vertex definitions. Specifically:

- Change Block 0 from `(0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2)` to **(2 6 7 3 1 5 4 0)**
- Change Block 1 from `(7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2)` to **(2 10 11 9 6 3 8 7)**

Alternatively, a simpler approach: reverse the entire vertex ordering for all blocks. The standard convention for OpenFOAM's `hex()` is that the first four vertices form one face and the last four form the opposite face, with the first face's normal pointing **away** from the second face. For Block 0, the correct ordering should be **(0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)** if we want the face `(0 1 2 3)` to be the "bottom" and `(4 5 6 7)` to be the "top".

However, since the user requires matching the squareBend tutorial geometry, the recommended fix is to swap the vertex ordering per block so that the face normals consistently point outward. For Block 0, use **(3 1 5 2 7 6 4 0)**, and for Block 1, use **(2 6 10 11 3 7 9 8)**.

---

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cannot find "points" file

**Error Message:**
```
Cannot find file "points" in directory "polyMesh"
```

**Diagnosis:**

This is a cascading error. Because `blockMesh` failed (due to the inside-out block), no mesh was generated. Therefore, the `constant/polyMesh/` directory doesn't exist yet, and `rhoSimpleFoam` cannot find the mesh.

**Fix:** This will resolve itself once the `blockMesh` error is fixed. After correcting the block vertex orderings in `blockMeshDict`, re-run `blockMesh` successfully, then run `rhoSimpleFoam`.

---

## Additional Issue: Boundary Name Mismatches

After fixing the mesh, there will likely be a mismatch between boundary patch names defined in `blockMeshDict` (`inlet`, `outlet`, `walls`) and those referenced in the `0/` field files (`inlet`, `outlet`, `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`).

**Recommendation:** Update the boundary patch names in `blockMeshDict` to match what the user requires. Rename the single `walls` patch into separate patches: `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`. Alternatively, consolidate all non-inlet/outlet walls into a single named patch called `walls` and update all `0/` field files accordingly.

Specifically in `blockMeshDict`, split the `walls` boundary into individual wall patches matching the `0/` files:
- `innerWall`
- `outerWall`
- `lowerWall`
- `upperWall`

Each should contain the appropriate face definitions from the current `walls` section.

Similarly, ensure the `0/T` file references these same patch names.

---

## Summary of Required Fixes

1. **`system/blockMeshDict`**: Correct the vertex ordering of all three hex blocks so they are not inside-out.
2. **`system/blockMeshDict`**: Split the combined `walls` patch into separate named patches (`innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`) to match the boundary conditions in the `0/` files.
3. Re-run `blockMesh`, then `rhoSimpleFoam`.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>
